purpose: Convert any input mesh into a voxel-grid representation — replaces smooth surface with regular 3D grid of voxel blocks. Two variants: Simple (default Cube) + Lego (artist-supplied Lego brick). Stateless (no memory@). The inverse of R10 (volume_pipeline_bridge — points to SDF), this goes mesh-to-voxel-blocks.
spenser_confirmation: 'this one is cool voxelizer it turns any object into voxel blocks - or legos here since its using custom geo'
'pulls color from the object into the blocks'
visual_result: Cache None at fresh load (LCV-rewire-on-load). Architecture confirmed via graph probe and Spenser's verbal description. UI Play would show Banana mesh replaced by grid of yellow Cubes (or yellow Lego bricks in V2), inheriting per-block color from the nearest banana surface point.
input_geometry: Banana (5100 PolygonObject) — any input mesh; user can drag custom geo into Source slot.

hosts.role: voxelize input mesh into Cube blocks (default)
voxelize input mesh into Lego brick blocks (artist-supplied alt block shape)

om_classic_generators.name: Connect
om_classic_generators.role: wraps Banana + Shader Field as host's input

om_classic_generators.name: Banana
om_classic_generators.role: input mesh — any custom geo can replace

om_classic_generators.name: Lego (Voxelizer_Lego only)
om_classic_generators.role: artist-supplied alt voxel block shape (the Lego brick)

key_nodes_in_graph.children@: OM children enumeration — reads Connect's children (Banana + Shader Field)
key_nodes_in_graph.fillgeometry@ Volume Voxel Fill: voxelizes input mesh into regular grid of cell positions — SAME PRIMITIVE as scene 04 Volume Infection. Universal voxelization primitive.
key_nodes_in_graph.nearestneighbor@ Closest Points: for each voxel center, find nearest input-mesh vertex (color sampling)
key_nodes_in_graph.cube@ Cube: default voxel block primitive (replaced with Lego mesh in V2)
key_nodes_in_graph.matrix@ + decomposematrix@: places each voxel block at its computed position
key_nodes_in_graph.color@ Color Op: per-voxel color emission (sampled from nearest mesh vertex × Shader Field)

am_params_named.name: Interior Filter
am_params_named.controls: which voxels count as 'inside' the input mesh

am_params_named.name: Children
am_params_named.controls: OM children reference for input mesh

what_is_clever: fillgeometry@ as universal voxelization primitive — same node serves point-cloud-to-SDF (scene 04) AND mesh-to-voxel-grid (scene 15). Dual-purpose.
Cube vs Lego variant via swappable block primitive — two side-by-side hosts demonstrate 'swap voxel shape' pattern. Generalizes to pebbles, hex tiles, custom shapes.
Shader Field + getvertexselectiondata + Color Op chain pulls per-voxel color from nearest input-mesh point. Texture-mapped meshes give texture-correct voxel colors automatically.
Stateless — same input + params = same output. No simulation, no memory@, no UI Play complexity (in theory; LCV-rewire-on-load issue is a separate framework gotcha).
Capsule-light form — 3-5 AM params is enough for clear single-purpose tools. Not every capsule needs Mycelium V3's 21 params.

minimal_reproducible_subgraph.name: mesh_to_voxel_blocks
minimal_reproducible_subgraph.purpose: Convert any mesh into a voxel-grid representation. Spawn N copies of a chosen block primitive at occupied voxel cells.
secondary_recipes: R50_swappable_voxel_block_shape — Lego/custom block via second OM-bound mesh
minimal_reproducible_subgraph.value_proposition: Minecraft / Lego / pixel-art / retro voxel-style look from any 3D mesh. Production technique for stylized animation. Generalizes to voxel-art games, blocky character variants, LEGO Movie aesthetic.
blockers_to_recipe_implementation: fillgeometry@ creation needs API surface (same blocker as R10/R17)
Swappable block-shape mesh binding needs Object-typed AM port
Per-voxel color sampling via nearestneighbor needs documenting

lessons_for_cinema4d_mcp: fillgeometry@ is the universal voxelization primitive — used across volume scenes 04, 14, 15, 16+.
Swappable-block-shape pattern (Cube → Lego → custom) is recipe-worthy.
Capsule-light style (3-5 AM params) is valid alongside capsule-rich (Mycelium V3's 21 params). Match exposure to scope.
Stateless capsule = drag-and-drop deterministic. No UI Play warmup needed once LCV-rewire-on-load is solved.

frames: frames/default.png — Banana visible via Connect, voxelization not engaged on fresh load
